text: EG Privacy Policy Overview The institute uses the e-Governance (EG) System to automate its academic and administrative processes. EG stores a significant amount of personal and official data of users. Poor practices at the end user level may result in data compromise or the loss of personal information. All users are responsible for taking the appropriate steps to review and secure their data within the application, as outlined below. Purpose This policy is meant to help you understand what information we collect, why we collect it, share information, and how you can update and manage your information. Scope The scope of this policy includes all users who have or are responsible for an account on the e-Governance system or have access to its portals. Policy ● In this privacy policy, the term "Personal Information" refers to any information from which the account holder's identity is apparent or can be reasonably ascertained. ● Generally, this application, while being used, does not col

agency may exercise a warrant to inspect the application or service provider's logs. ● EG does not track the location information. If a browser application asks for a location, one may disable or block it. ● This web application uses cookies for session management. A cookie is a piece of software code that an internet website sends to the browser when people access information at the site. ● Email address and mobile number will not be used for other purposes than to contact and communicate with people. ● For most administrative requirements, Information stored in the application will be used and will not be asked through other means such as email or forms. People are advised to keep their information updated, which is necessary for communication, career progression, and contribution to various services. ● As an account holder, one must not share any information about other people (accessible on EG) without the explicit approval of the competent authority. # Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- About Us
- Team

In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an integrated e-Governance Solution (EGS) to cater to all the processes and workflows with a vision of an almost paperless office. Entire workflow automation is achieved through an organic, bottom-up iterative process with end-user participation.

Over the past six years, our people have recognised the outstanding achievements of EGS in improving institutess ability to create and evolve high-quality software-dependent systems. However, human touch is essential. EGS has demonstrated an exceptional degree of significant, measured, sustained, and shared productivity improvements by composing a symphony of organically designed wo

- The list of features provisioned in EGS is huge, along with 140 workflows and several other transactional interfaces. The system is supported by robust reports, dashboards for ready references (summary data), and user messaging channels, including email, SMS, and Telegram integration.
- Processes were defined, matured, and optimized over time. Workflows were defined, iterated, and improved to help us over time. Now, it is fairly easy to add another workflow or report!
- A lot of effort has gone into the design, application building blocks, thought process, and customization with rigour. Our early investment in the e-Governance Solution, a continued thrust, and much-needed support from the administration have helped us to witness a digital transformation of processes - it's right here in front of us. Director IIT Jammu, an exponent of digital infrastructure for institutes, has been very supportive and inspiring and has encouraged us at each stage. EGS keeps the campus and people conne

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

IIT Jammu Intranet portal, provisioned in 2020, provides some useful information such as minutes of meetings of statutory committees, previous annual reports, Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) for collaborations with other institutes or organizations, some office-specific documents, checklists, etc. meant for internal circulation. Intranet portal is accessible from within the campus. Hence the name. To access Intranet portal, please visit **intranet.iitjammu.ac.in** from within the campus network.

# Page Title: IIT Jammu SARAL - Student Portal

### About Us

In July 2018, IIT Jammu embarked upon an ambitious journey to simplify its Academic, Research, and Administrative processes (Academic Affairs, Student Affairs, HR, Accounts and Audit, R and C, Procurement, CEP, etc.). Within a year, we built an integrated e-Governance Solution (EGS) to cater to all the processes and workflows with a vision of an almost paperless office. Entire workflow automation is achieved through an organic, bottom-up iterative process with end-user participation.

### Message

EG effortlessly established accountability for service delivery and functional elegance in the very roots of the campus activities.

##### Suman Sarkar
Associate Dean - Digital Infrastructure (SARAL)
